messages.content: Hey, I'm working on Homework 1 and I'm not sure what machine types to pick for my architecture design. Can you help?

Of course! So for Homework 1, you're designing compute infrastructure for a hypothetical web application. The key is matching machine types to your workload. For a typical web app, E2 or N2 instances are usually a great fit. E2 is the most cost-effective for dev and test, while N2 gives you a better balance of price and performance for production workloads.
What about if part of the app does image processing? Would I need something different?

Good thinking! For image processing, you'd want compute-optimized instances like the C2 family. They have the highest per-core performance, which is great for CPU-intensive workloads like batch processing or rendering. You could use C2 instances for the image processing tier and E2 or N2 for the web frontend. That kind of tiered approach is exactly what the homework is looking for.
Got it. And the homework asks for a cost estimate. How do I figure that out?

Use the Google Cloud Pricing Calculator at cloud.google.com/products/calculator. You can plug in your machine types, the number of instances, hours per month, and it'll give you an estimate. Don't forget to factor in persistent disk costs too. Also mention in your write-up whether you're using preemptible or Spot VMs for any batch workloads — they can save you sixty to ninety percent over regular pricing.

Homework 1 is due Week 4, Thursday by eleven fifty-nine PM Pacific. It's worth 50 points. You need to submit an architecture diagram, a two-page written justification explaining your choices, and a cost estimate spreadsheet. Upload everything to Canvas. You still have a bit of time, but I'd recommend starting the diagram soon!
